Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Simple This scenario outlines a basic cyber attack campaign targeting the energy sector, utilizing phishing techniques for initial access followed by execution of malicious files, culminating in defacement impacting network traffic.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send phishing emails with malicious attachments or links to trick employees into downloading and executing them.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Upon opening the malicious attachment, the reg.exe utility executes the embedded commands, leading to unauthorized actions on the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The compromised system is used to change the content of legitimate web pages or device interfaces, displaying defacement messages or images.

Scenario.summary: The Unitronics Defacement Campaign employs basic phishing techniques to gain initial access to energy sector networks, subsequently executing malicious files to achieve defacement, disrupting normal operations and potentially causing reputational damage.
